tldr: These tools solve different problems — Hyprland is a Linux Wayland compositor and Slint is a cross-platform declarative GUI toolkit — so most users will not migrate but instead replace use-cases on a feature-by-feature basis.
why_switch: You no longer need a desktop compositor and instead want to build native GUI apps
You want a portable UI toolkit that runs on Linux, macOS, Windows, embedded, and web
You prefer a declarative UI language with live preview over compositor scripting
You want bindings for Rust, C++, JavaScript, and Python rather than compositor IPC
Your project shifted from a Linux desktop environment to a shipped GUI product
what_to_export: List of custom keybindings and workflows
Plugin list and any custom plugin source
Any scripts that interact with hyprctl
export_method: Hyprland stores everything in plain-text config files under ~/.config/hypr/. Copy hyprland.conf and any sourced files. There is no proprietary export — your config IS the export. Consult official Hyprland wiki for the current config schema.
import_method: Slint does not import compositor configs. You build a Slint application from scratch using the .slint declarative language and bindings for Rust, C++, JS, or Python. Start with the official Slint getting-started guide and the slint-ui/slint repo examples. There is no automated migration path between these tools.

concept_mapping.note: Both are declarative text but for entirely different domains — translate by hand only where concepts overlap.

concept_mapping.target: Slint API calls from host language
concept_mapping.note: Slint is controlled from Rust/C++/JS/Python — not via IPC sockets.
Set via Window {} component properties; consult Slint docs for current property names.
Reusable UI pieces are authored as components, not loaded as plugins.
Slint has built-in animation primitives; check official docs for syntax.
Implemented in host code, not in the .slint file.

effort: Not directly comparable — rebuilding a workflow takes 1-3 days; full app development is open-ended.
gotchas: These tools are not substitutes — confirm Slint actually solves your problem before starting
Slint dual-licensing (GPL + commercial) may matter for closed-source projects — check current license terms
No automated migration tooling exists between the two
Slint's ecosystem is smaller than mature toolkits like Qt — verify needed widgets exist
Embedded targets need MCU-specific Slint setup; consult official docs

email.subject: Moving from Hyprland to Slint — heads up
email.body:
Hi team,

Quick note: Hyprland (Wayland compositor) and Slint (GUI toolkit) solve different problems, so this isn't a like-for-like migration. We're treating it as a use-case shift rather than a port. I'll archive the hyprland.conf for reference and bootstrap a Slint project from the official examples. Will share a plan once I scope the work.

Thanks
